doc_name: FDA Shares Becton Dickinson’s Letter About BD Alaris Pumps That Have Been Dropped or Jarred | Oncology Nursing Society

chunks.doc_name: FDA Shares Becton Dickinson’s Letter About BD Alaris Pumps That Have Been Dropped or Jarred | Oncology Nursing Society
chunks.text: # FDA Shares Becton Dickinson’s Letter About BD Alaris Pumps That Have Been Dropped or Jarred ## Breadcrumb BD said that dropping the Alaris pump module may damage the bezel assembly, which provides the mechanical foundation for critical pumping components. The damage, which would be internal and not readily visible, can cause underinfusion, overinfusion, unregulated flow, or failure to calibrate. ![FDA Update 2024](/sites/default/files/styles/responsive_4_3_400w/public/2024-11/fdaupdate.jpg.webp?h=c6c1fbd8&itok=FS-oschI) On November 28, 2025, the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) [announced that Becton Dickinson (BD) had issued a letter](https://www.fda.gov/medical-devices/medical-device-recalls-and-early-alerts/correction-alert-becton-dickinson-updates-use-instructions-bd-alaris-pump-issue) to affected customers recommending users take additional precautions with Alaris™ pump modules that have been dropped or severely jarred. BD said that dropping the Alaris pump module may damage the bezel assembly, which provides the mechanical foundation for critical pumping components. The damage, which would be internal and not readily visible, can cause underinfusion, overinfusion, unregulated flow, or failure to calibrate. “Damage to the pumping mechanism may prevent the device from delivering an accurate amount of fluid flow through the pumping cycle resulting in an over or under infusion condition,” [BD said](https://www.fda.gov/medical-devices/medical-device-recalls-and-early-alerts/correction-alert-becton-dickinson-updates-use-instructions-bd-alaris-pump-issue). “Over- and underinfusions can potentially lead to serious adverse health consequences including serious injury and death, depending on the type of drug (and its resulting clinical effect), the patient criticality and magnitude of the flow rate inaccuracy.” The FDA [identified it as a class I recall](https://www.fda.gov/medical-devices/medical-device-recalls-and-early-alerts/correction-alert-becton-dickinson-updates-use-instructions-bd-alaris-pump-issue), the most serious type, where continued use of a damaged device could cause serious injury or death. As of September 22, 2025, BD has reported two serious injuries and no deaths associated with the issue. The recall affects the following devices: | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | **Product** | **Catalog (Ref) Number** | **UDI-DI/GTIN** | | BD Alaris pump module | 8100 | [10885403810015](https://accessgudid.nlm.nih.gov/devices/10885403810015) [10885403810046](https://accessgudid.nlm.nih.gov/devices/10885403810046) [10885403810039](https://accessgudid.nlm.nih.gov/devices/10885403810039) [10885403222054](https://accessgudid.nlm.nih.gov/devices/10885403222054) [10885403517723](https://accessgudid.nlm.nih.gov/devices/10885403517723) 10885403213212 10885403211423 | | Kit, bezel assembly 8100bd | 49000437 | 10885403471018 | | Kit assembly one piece bezel 8100 | 49000270 | 10885403247743 | On October 17, 2025, BD sent all affected customers a letter recommending the following actions: U.S. customers with questions about the recall can use the following contact information: Healthcare professionals and consumers should also report adverse reactions or quality problems they experienced using the devices to [MedWatch: The FDA Safety Information and Adverse Event Reporting Program](https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/scripts/medwatch/index.cfm?action=reporting.home). ONS membership connects you with expert-driven support and resources to provide essential, quality care. © 2026 Oncology Nursing Society ![](https://px.ads.linkedin.com/collect/?pid=8345828&fmt=gif) ![](https://www.facebook.com/tr?id=1594859944895191 &ev=PageView&noscript=1)
